comment: Tests: multi-subject extraction, journalist edge, clear distinct relation types (criticize/support/resign).
title: Sam Altman Returns to OpenAI as CEO
author: Jane Doe
body_text: Sam Altman has returned to OpenAI as CEO after a brief but dramatic firing by the board. Elon Musk, an early investor and co-founder of OpenAI, publicly criticized the board's decision to remove Altman. Satya Nadella, the CEO of Microsoft, expressed strong support for Altman and offered him a senior role at Microsoft. Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president, resigned in solidarity with Altman shortly after the firing. Altman and Brockman were later reinstated following significant pressure from OpenAI employees and investors.

comment: Tests: surface-form variation (last-name-only Musk/Altman), legal relation vocabulary.
title: Musk Sues OpenAI and Altman Over Mission Drift
author: Mary Smith
body_text: Elon Musk has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman, accusing the company of abandoning its founding non-profit mission. Musk co-founded OpenAI alongside Altman in 2015 but departed from the board in 2018. The complaint alleges that Altman partnered with Microsoft in a way that betrayed the original charter. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has not publicly responded to the suit.

comment: Tests org filtering: corporate-subject sentences where some have named humans responsible and some don't. OpenAI / Microsoft / Google / Anthropic must NEVER appear in people. The Pichai→Anthropic edge must be DROPPED (no Anthropic leader named).
title: OpenAI and Microsoft Extend Their Partnership
author: Pat Lee
body_text: OpenAI and Microsoft announced an expanded partnership today, with Microsoft committing additional capital to fund OpenAI's next training run. Sam Altman, OpenAI's CEO, said the deal will give the company more compute. Satya Nadella, Microsoft's CEO, framed the extension as a long-term commitment to enterprise AI. Google, conspicuously absent from the talks, has been investing in Anthropic instead. Sundar Pichai, who leads Google, has not commented publicly.

relationships.note: partnership is symmetric — either direction acceptable; gold uses the LLM's preferred ordering

comment: Same last name (Amodei): Dario and Daniela. Tests that the LLM uses full canonical names for both siblings and doesn't leak a bare 'Amodei' as a person, even though the body mixes first-name and bare references. The extractor eval only scores LLM extraction quality; the resolver's recency disambiguation is exercised end-to-end by tests/test_resolver.py.
title: Amodei Siblings Lay Out Anthropic's Year-Ahead Roadmap
author: Sarah Chen
body_text: Anthropic co-founders Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei together unveiled the company's roadmap for the coming year on stage at TechCrunch Disrupt. Dario, who leads Anthropic as CEO, walked through technical milestones for the next Claude model. Daniela, the company's president, then laid out plans for responsible scaling and enterprise growth. Daniela also confirmed that headcount would expand significantly in the second half of the year. Sarah Chen reports that the siblings' joint appearance was widely seen as a deliberate show of unity ahead of intensifying competition with OpenAI.

comment: Same first name (Anthony): Anthony Lopez (subject) and Anthony Ha (journalist/author). Body uses 'Lopez' and full names; tests that the LLM uses full canonical names and doesn't emit a bare 'Anthony' as a person.
title: Anthony Lopez Launches AI-First Email Client
author: Anthony Ha
body_text: Anthony Lopez, a former Google product lead, unveiled his new venture today: an AI-first email client called Trama. Lopez said the product addresses what he called the 'attention tax' of modern inboxes. Anthony Ha, who has been covering consumer AI for TechCrunch since 2018, attended the launch event in San Francisco. Lopez confirmed that Sequoia Capital led a $14 million seed round; Roelof Botha was named as the deal partner. Anthony Ha later reported that Trama's waitlist had crossed 50,000 signups within hours of the announcement.

comment: Tests: investor/founder vocabulary, distinguishing 'invests in' from 'leads'.
title: Anthropic Raises $4B with Amazon as Lead Investor
author: Alex Chen
body_text: Anthropic, the AI safety company co-founded by Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei, has closed a $4 billion funding round led by Amazon. Andy Jassy, Amazon's CEO, said the investment reflects the company's commitment to generative AI. Dario Amodei previously worked at OpenAI under Sam Altman before founding Anthropic. The round positions Anthropic as a direct competitor to OpenAI in the foundation model market.

relationships.note: article literally says 'Dario Amodei previously worked at OpenAI under Sam Altman' — direction follows the article's framing
